what_it_does: This file is a ledger record for a generated image asset named keystone in the provider-registry channel; it stores YAML frontmatter metadata (id, address, blob references, MIME type, authorship, timestamps, and an inline version history) plus a one-line body that repeats the latest version's alt text.
observations: YAML frontmatter with a complete metadata block for an image asset
Two versions (v1 and v2) are recorded inline, each with its own blob://b2 hash, MIME image/jpeg, and alt text
Width and height are both 0 despite the asset being an image
The source is claude_code and the author_session is ch-z4ht5ipb
links is empty and the body contains only 'v2 — richer'
standouts: Version history is embedded directly in the ledger file rather than in separate files
Dimensions are 0x0 for an image/jpeg record
Alt text doubles as version annotation and file body
concerns: w and h are 0, so the record does not describe the actual image size
links is empty, giving no context about where or how the image is used
Version descriptions are informal ('v1 attempt', 'v2 — richer') and not machine-semantic
notes: A generated keystone image ledger entry with two blob versions and minimal descriptive metadata.
questions: Why are width and height recorded as 0?
What does 'keystone' refer to in this system?
Where is the blob physically stored and how is blob://b2 resolved?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to catalog a generated image asset and its versions; it does record identity, blob references, and version notes, but it fails to record real dimensions or usage relationships, so it catalogs rather than fully describes the asset.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a stable, addressable record of a generated image so it can be referenced by an image:// URI, while preserving the history of blob revisions and human-readable version notes.
novelty: The ledger combines a channel registry entry, blob addressing, and a full inline version history annotated with alt-text notes in a single markdown file.
contribution: Provides a versioned, addressable metadata record for a generated image asset, including blob references and human-readable version notes, which a fused system could keep for provenance and asset lookup.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown ledger file recording metadata and two blob versions of a generated JPEG image named keystone in the provider-registry channel.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and preserve the identity, provenance, and revision history of a generated image asset under a stable image:// address.
